headline: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator from accessing Claude
angle: A company bans someone from using their product because that person built something too popular with their product, creating a perfect microcosm of tech's self-sabotage.
scene: A developer sitting at a desk, staring at a laptop screen showing an error message. Behind them on the wall is a framed certificate that says 'Employee of the Month' with a strikethrough. On the desk: empty coffee cups, a stress ball, and a printed terms-of-service document folded into a paper airplane.
setup: After his tool got too popular, Anthropic banned him from using Claude.
punchline: Turns out there's a clause in the user agreement: 'Thou shalt not succeed.'
instagram_caption: Nothing says we support developers like banning the ones who actually build something people want.
image_prompt: Single-panel cartoon, clean line art, thick black outlines, muted blue-gray pastel colors, white background, New Yorker magazine aesthetic, flat colors, no shading, no gradients, no signature. A tired developer at a desk, looking at a laptop screen displaying an error or ban notification. The workspace shows signs of frustration—crumpled papers, empty coffee cups, a stress ball. Behind the desk, a framed certificate on the wall with a strikethrough mark. The overall mood is one of ironic disappointment and bureaucratic absurdity.
